perturbation.original_text: 14. Minimum Purchase Obligation The Distributor agrees to purchase from Lucid minimum agreed quantity of product in the first, second and third years of the Agreement, excluding demonstration product. These quantities to be agreed by both parties in the initial detailed sales plan noted in clause 9D and amended with further agreement in subsequent annual sales plans
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Minimum Purchase Obligation The Distributor agrees to purchase from Lucid minimum agreed quantity of product in the first, second and third years of the Agreement, excluding demonstration product.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the part 'These quantities to be agreed by both parties in the initial detailed sales plan noted in clause 9D and amended with further agreement in subsequent annual sales plans', the minimum purchase obligation becomes vague and unenforceable. Section 9D references a 'detailed sales plan' but without clause 14 directly linking purchase quantities to this sales plan, the obligation lacks a defined metric. This creates an in-text contradiction because clause 9D depends on clause 14 for specifics.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D) The Distributor agrees to supply Lucid, on an annual basis, with a detailed sales plan for the Product(s), broken down by sales for each individual country within the Territory. Any country or part of the territory that does not meet the sales expectations as forecasted by the Distributor will be subject to review by Lucid and may subsequently be removed from the Territory. The first sales plan will be presented to Lucid within three (3) months of the signing of this agreement and annual sales plans will be due on November 30 each year.
perturbation.justification: The original text of clause 14 specified that minimum purchase quantities would be agreed upon in the sales plan detailed in clause 9D. The changed text removes this link, making the minimum purchase obligation vague and unenforceable. Clause 9D outlines the sales plan requirements. Without clause 14 linking purchase quantities to the sales plan, it is unclear how the 'minimum agreed quantity' is determined, creating a contradiction between the undefined obligation in clause 14 and the expectation in clause 9D that the sales plan will inform purchase obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 15. PAYMENT TERMS The Distributor agrees to abide by Lucid's standard payment terms, which are as follows; One Hundred (100) per cent of order value by sixty ( 90 ) day irrevocable letter of credit when order is placed on Lucid.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. PAYMENT TERMS The Distributor agrees to abide by Lucid's standard payment terms.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'which are as follows; One Hundred (100) per cent of order value by sixty ( 90 ) day irrevocable letter of credit when order is placed on Lucid.', the specific payment terms are omitted, making the payment obligation undefined. If Lucid's 'standard terms and conditions of sale' are mentioned in section 13 B, but these terms are not explicitly defined in the agreement itself, this omission introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 15 says there are payment terms, but does not specify them, and they are not elsewhere in the document either.
perturbation.contradicted_text: B) All orders from the Distributor to Lucid will be subject to Lucid's standard terms and conditions of sale.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 15 specified payment terms. The changed text removes these specific terms, stating only that the Distributor agrees to Lucid's standard payment terms. The contradicted text in Section B states that all orders are subject to Lucid's standard terms and conditions of sale. The problem is that without the payment terms being defined in Section 15, and no payment terms defined anywhere else, it contradicts the idea that payment terms are agreed upon. This creates a contradiction because while Section B refers to 'Lucid's standard terms and conditions', these terms are not actually defined in the document. The removal creates a contradiction because it makes the payment obligations undefined in the contract, despite section B referencing standard terms.

perturbation.original_text: 18. Incident and Traceability Reporting A) DISTRIBUTOR will IMMEDIATELY report via telephone communication to Lucid any incidents that involve the use of PRODUCTS sold by the DISTRIBUTOR to end users that affect patient safety or well-being (an "INCIDENT"). Such oral incident reporting will be followed immediately by a written incident report that documents the detailed conditions that resulted in the INCIDENT and Distributor shall thereafter co-operate with Lucid in the investigation of any and all conditions that led to, or resulted from the INCIDENT.
perturbation.changed_text: 18. Incident and Traceability Reporting A) DISTRIBUTOR will IMMEDIATELY report via telephone communication to Lucid any incidents that involve the use of PRODUCTS sold by the DISTRIBUTOR to end users that affect patient safety or well-being (an "INCIDENT").
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Such oral incident reporting will be followed immediately by a written incident report that documents the detailed conditions that resulted in the INCIDENT and Distributor shall thereafter co-operate with Lucid in the investigation of any and all conditions that led to, or resulted from the INCIDENT.', the obligation to provide a written incident report and cooperate in the investigation is removed. This creates a contradiction within Section 18 itself as the initial sentence mandates reporting of incidents (an INCIDENT), implying a follow-up process. By not detailing that process, it creates an inconsistency.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A) DISTRIBUTOR will IMMEDIATELY report via telephone communication to Lucid any incidents that involve the use of PRODUCTS sold by the DISTRIBUTOR to end users that affect patient safety or well-being (an "INCIDENT").
perturbation.justification: The original text specified both oral and written reports for incidents, along with cooperation in investigations. The changed text removes the requirement for a written report and cooperation, directly contradicting the implied expectation of a follow-up process after the initial oral report. The contradicted text highlights the initial reporting requirement (an 'INCIDENT'), which is now inconsistent without the mandated follow-up process outlined in the original text but removed in the changed text. Thus, the omission creates a direct contradiction.
